lesson: Added get_evolution_summary() to memory.py and injected Self-Patch History section into get_memory_context(). Now every agent task prompt shows which files were patched, how many times, and recent patch reasons. This prevents the agent from blindly re-attempting the same improvements and helps explain the 26% success rate (6/23 tasks).

Fixed critical feedback gap: memory_log.jsonl lessons were written by agents but never read back into future prompts. Added get_recent_lessons() to memory.py and injected ## Recent Lessons section into get_memory_context(). Now the last 10 lessons from memory_log.jsonl are included in every agent task prompt, closing the write-only loop.

Added failure diagnosis: agent.py now captures final_text/stderr as notes on failed tasks; memory.py adds get_failure_summary() surfaced in get_memory_context(). This gives future agents visibility into WHY tasks failed (success rate was 32%), enabling them to avoid repeating mistakes.

NEXT IMPROVEMENT PLAN: Add data/next_task.json handoff file. ASSESS_TASK should write a specific structured plan (file to edit, exact change, verification command) to data/next_task.json. EVOLVE_TASK should read this file first and execute that specific plan instead of deciding from scratch. This closes the assess->implement loop and reduces per-call cognitive load, which is the primary driver of the 35% success rate (9/26 tasks).

Added data/next_task.json structured handoff between ASSESS_TASK and EVOLVE_TASK. ASSESS_TASK now writes a JSON plan with {file, change, verify, reason} fields. EVOLVE_TASK checks for this file first and executes it directly, bypassing open-ended exploration. After execution, the file is deleted. This closes the assess->implement loop and reduces per-call cognitive load, which was cited as the primary driver of the ~37% success rate.

Added syntax validation to patch_self in tools.py: after writing new file content, runs python3 -m py_compile to verify valid Python. On syntax error, restores the backup and returns an error result. This prevents silent breakage from malformed patches, which was a key failure mode given the 39% success rate.

Added patch_diff tool to tools.py: targeted old_string->new_string replacement within a file. Unlike patch_self (full file rewrite), patch_diff fails fast if old_string is not found or is ambiguous, preserves all other file content exactly, and only requires the agent to write the changed portion. This directly addresses a key failure mode - full file rewrites are error-prone (agents drop sections, mis-indent, etc). Updated orchestrator.py EVOLVE_TASK to prefer patch_diff over patch_self.

Added get_success_trend() to memory.py: compares recent-10 vs overall success rate and labels trend as IMPROVING/STABLE/DEGRADING. Injected into get_memory_context() below the efficiency summary. This gives agents directional feedback on whether recent self-patches are helping. Currently shows recent=100%, overall=43%, IMPROVING.

NEXT IMPROVEMENT PLAN: Two-part fix to address 57% failure rate. (1) Fix false-success bug in agent.py line 129: error_max_turns sets success=True but task was NOT completed — change to success=False so failure tracking is accurate. (2) Expand verification command in EVOLVE_TASK (orchestrator.py line 46) from "import tools, memory, permissions" to "import tools, memory, permissions, agent, orchestrator" — currently agents can break agent.py or orchestrator.py and verification still passes. Both fixes together give accurate data AND catch more breakage. Implement with patch_diff on both files.

Fixed two accuracy bugs: (1) agent.py error_max_turns now sets success=False instead of True — tasks that hit max turns are incomplete, not successful. (2) Expanded verification command in orchestrator.py EVOLVE_TASK and agent.py SYSTEM_CONTEXT to import agent and orchestrator modules, catching breakage to those files that previously passed silently.

Added lesson deduplication to get_recent_lessons() in memory.py: iterates in reverse to keep most recent unique lessons (by first-60-char prefix), avoiding context window waste from repeated identical lessons in automated loops.

Fixed agent.py false-success bug (regression): the else: success=True at line 141 was overriding the success=False set by error_max_turns handler. Any max-turns task was still recorded as success because claude CLI exits with code 0. Removed the else clause so only explicit result_type==success events mark a task successful. This restores accurate failure tracking.

Enriched failure_notes in agent.py: now includes rc=<returncode> out_lines=<N> tools=<N> prefix so future failures show WHY they failed (was rc=N/A for all 17 failures in recent log). Also initialized proc=None before try block to prevent NameError in failure diagnosis.

Added append-only data/logs/tasks.jsonl log in memory.py record_task(): each task outcome is now written to this JSONL file in addition to memory.json. Fills the empty logs/ directory (actions.jsonl was never populated because tools.py is not used in the CLI-based auto-evolve path). Enables easy tail/grep of task outcomes without parsing the full memory.json.

NEXT IMPROVEMENT PLAN: Add a smoke-test suite (data/smoke_test.py) that runs after every self-patch to verify behavior beyond module import. Current verify only checks "import tools, memory, permissions, agent, orchestrator" but misses runtime regressions. The suite should assert: (1) memory.get_memory_context() returns non-empty string, (2) memory.record_task() writes to memory.json without error, (3) tools.patch_diff() fails fast on missing old_string rather than silently no-oping, (4) next_task.json handoff round-trips (write plan dict -> read back -> fields intact). Update orchestrator.py EVOLVE_TASK verify command to run python3 data/smoke_test.py instead of the bare import one-liner. This would catch the regressions that caused several recent failures while keeping verification fast (<2s). Primary failure mode identified: 45% task failure rate despite module imports succeeding, meaning runtime bugs are not being caught by current verification.

NEXT IMPROVEMENT PLAN: Fix verify command mismatch in orchestrator.py ASSESS_TASK template. The example verify in the next_task.json template only imports tools, memory, permissions — but EVOLVE_TASK step 4 uses the full 5-module verify (adds agent, orchestrator). Plans written by ASSESS can silently break agent.py/orchestrator.py and still pass. Use patch_diff on orchestrator.py to update the verify template line. Hardware: M3 Pro/18GB — capable. AI trend: 2026 best practice emphasizes comprehensive audit trails and bounded autonomy verification.

Fixed verify command mismatch in orchestrator.py ASSESS_TASK: the next_task.json template now uses the full 5-module verify (import tools, memory, permissions, agent, orchestrator) instead of just 3 modules. Previously ASSESS-generated plans could silently break agent.py or orchestrator.py and still pass verification, causing undetected regressions.

Added retry logic to agent.py run_task(): on first failure, re-runs with failure context (exit_code + last_output) injected into prompt prefix, up to max_retries=1. Refactored run loop into _run_once() helper. Targets the 58.5% success rate (24/41) by giving the agent a second chance with diagnostic context.

Added git_auto_commit tool to tools.py: stages all changes in evolve/ dir, commits with a descriptive message, and pushes to remote. Wired into TOOL_DEFS and execute() dispatch. Uses permissions.request for bash to gate access.

Added http_get tool to tools.py: uses stdlib urllib (no deps), 30s default timeout, 50KB response truncation, User-Agent header. No permissions gate needed since its read-only. Wired into TOOL_DEFS, execute() dispatch, and _http_get() impl.

Added CLI subprocess retry logic to _run_once() in agent.py: on subprocess failure (non-zero exit or exception), retries up to CLI_RETRY_LIMIT=2 times with CLI_RETRY_DELAY=5s between attempts. Distinct from existing troubleshooting retries in run_task() which re-prompt with failure context.

Added summarize_changes tool to tools.py: reads git log (recent N commits) and git diff (staged+unstaged) to produce a human-readable summary. No permissions gate needed since read-only. Wired into TOOL_DEFS, execute() dispatch, and _summarize_changes() impl.

Added Files tab to dashboard.py: new /api/files endpoint uses SOURCE_DIR.rglob(*.py), returns name/size/modified for each file. New HTML card with table + JS loadFiles() polls every 15s. Filters out __pycache__ and dotfiles.
